summary: Thomas came in my class today with Jamie, Nicola, Meilin and Élise. Thomas and Meilin were very attentive and did what they were asked, and it was great. Nicola, Thomas and Thomas could really show that they understood the vocabulary, and I was really proud of their work. They could all tell their moods, their genders, and they could also tell the weather. I was really proud, and they could do the gestures for the weather, which was great.
The group was a bit tired. Thomas could do the test very well. He did the "Let's go to the zoo" activity, but not with the gestures like the others.
(Thomas was not there). They all loved the 'Why are you so sad' story, and they could repeat the phrases very well.
It was the day of the test. Thomas was not there.

Thomas could not tell the animal but could tell the colour, and he needed help to tell me the animal. He could tell the genders and the moods, needed a bit of help to remember the full structure at times, could pair the numbers with the spots, could count very well, could repeat the weather and do the gestures, and could repeat the full phrase for "Why are you so sad." He was not overly participative, and I had to carry him to the group for the routines. He seemed to love the activity.

My Sam Saturday students were rather enthusiastic. Thomas was a bit uncooperative at times. They all could pair up their dot cards with the right number. Apart from Liam, they all could tell the animal on their cards, they all could count well. Hasham did need a little help to guess his animal and count. They all loved the spots and stripes version 2 activity. Apart from Thomas, they all loved the Let's go to the zoo. I had to tell them not to bend the cards and after several remarks, I told them that I was very angry and took the cards from some of them. Thomas and Marius refused to highfive me after but the others were okay. They all enjoyed story time.
